headline: Broadcom Aims To Shape AI Data Centers With Open OCI Standard
summary: Broadcom (NasdaqGS:AVGO) is helping lead a new open Optical Compute Interconnect (OCI) standard for AI data centers alongside AMD, Meta, Microsoft, Nvidia, and OpenAI. The company is expanding its end to end AI data center ecosystem through optical and liquid cooling partnerships, including work with JetCool and Flex. Broadcom is using its own co packaged optics and connectivity hardware to support OCI, aiming to move AI infrastructure toward multi supplier, interoperable...

headline: CNBC: ByteDance building AI chip capacity outside China using Nvidia Blackwell

headline: AMD Places Bet On Open Optical Standards For Future AI Data Centers
summary: AMD (NasdaqGS:AMD) and partners including Broadcom, Meta, Microsoft, NVIDIA and OpenAI have formed the Optical Compute Interconnect (OCI) Multi-Source Agreement consortium. The group aims to define open standards for optical interconnects in AI data centers, with a focus on improvements in scalability, power efficiency and performance. The OCI MSA focuses on moving AI infrastructure away from traditional copper connectivity toward optical links that are suited for large AI models and...

The OCI MSA focuses on moving AI infrastructure away from traditional copper connectivity toward optical links that are suited for large AI models and...

headline: The Iran War Could Upend AI. Here’s How.

headline: 2 Mega-Cap Stocks to Keep an Eye On and 1 Facing Headwinds
summary: Megacap stocks are behemoths that set the tone for their industries, and their massive scale typically leads to wide moats. However, the downside is that most have already exploited their existing market opportunities and must invest heavily to expand further, a risky proposition.

headline: More Pain at the Pump | Heard on the Street Recap
summary: What Happened in Markets Today Oil prices stay high. Brent crude picked up another 2.7% to stay above the $100 mark on Friday. This despite the U.S. loosening some restrictions on the purchase of Russian oil in an effort to ease the global supply crunch.
